behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (request builder -> API client payload). A pure Unit test of a helper could miss wiring bugs where the sent payload drops labels. REAL-INFRA is not required because an in-process fake client can capture outbound payload exactly. Rejected higher levels: no behavior text requiring real HTTP/OS/subprocess, and correctness does not depend on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake API client that records the dispatched request; call classify() with a taxonomy and assert the outbound payload contains the full label list every time.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is about end-to-end handling of provider output through response parsing/normalization to caller return value. A Unit test of normalization alone can miss integration defects (wrong field mapped, normalization bypassed). No real infra is needed; fake API response is sufficient. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not require real provider variability, only invalid-label handling.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client returning an out-of-taxonomy label; assert classify() returns "unknown" and never surfaces the raw unexpected label.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly one local deterministic transformation (normalization) with identical inputs. No module boundary interaction is required to expose the failure. Rejected higher levels: behavior text scopes to "normalization step" and identical raw responses, which disqualifies System/Agentic/Workflow needs.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text requires "repeated live API calls" and label consistency contingent on provider response changes, so correctness depends on real model/provider outputs and their variability. Mocks would hide this by returning constants. Rejected Workflow: this is repeated single-step classification checks, not a multi-stage agent pipeline with distinct model decisions across stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classification calls against the live provider for the same document/taxonomy, record raw provider outputs and final labels, and assert label changes only when provider output changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is between budget enforcement logic and dispatch control (truncate/reject before send). Unit tests of token math alone can miss actual pre-dispatch gating. In-process fake client can verify whether dispatch happened and with what payload, so System is unnecessary. Rejected Agentic: no dependence on model judgment/output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small token budget, invoke classify() with over-budget input, and assert policy behavior (truncate or reject) occurs before dispatch by inspecting fake client call count/payload.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Assertion depends on real provider-reported usage from a real AI API call path; stubs can mask tokenizer/model-side accounting differences and always pass. Per rule, real AI API execution with correctness tied to provider/model-returned values defaults to Agentic. Rejected Workflow: only single-call budget compliance, not multi-step agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real provider calls for representative documents, read provider-reported token usage metadata, and assert usage <= configured per-call budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This tests orchestration/retry behavior across classifier + client + backoff logic. A fake client can deterministically return rate-limit then success to expose real retry/state bugs; real network is not required. Rejected System: behavior text requires handling of rate-limit signal semantics, not real transport effects. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: correctness is retry control flow, not model output quality or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client scripted to return rate-limit on first call and success on second; assert classify() waits/backs off, retries, and returns success instead of surfacing an error.
